data.title: Yolo Mode — No Confirm/Reject/Human Flow
data.rationale: The agent always runs in yolo mode — no confirm/reject/human flow required, which significantly simplifies both the AILANG brain and the TypeScript frontend. Eliminates the Mode ADT entirely.

data.title: Option D Model Selection via call_with(SharedMem)
data.rationale: A new call_with(model, prompt) builtin reads the current model from SharedMem, so /model in the TUI switches provider mid-session without restarting the brain. Until implemented, a fallback reads from env var.

data.title: Three-Process Architecture (TUI ↔ AILANG Brain ↔ Env Server)
data.rationale: Three processes, two protocols. TypeScript owns terminal and environment server. AILANG brain is child process over JSONL stdin/stdout. Environment server embedded in TypeScript process — no Python dependency.

data.title: JSONL Protocol for Inter-Process Communication
data.rationale: Deliberately minimal. AILANG writes events to stdout; TypeScript writes commands to AILANG stdin. All records newline-delimited JSON. Split on \n only. Event types: session_start, thinking, proposed_cmd, obs, done, error. Commands: abort, model_change.

data.title: TypeScript Environment Server Replaces Python
data.rationale: The environment server is embedded in the TypeScript process using express + child_process.execSync. POST /exec, /snapshot, /restore, GET /health. Removes Python dependency from the stack.

data.title: _io_poll_stdin Builtin for Non-Blocking Stdin Peek
data.rationale: Small new builtin (non-blocking stdin peek, returns empty string if nothing ready). ~10 lines in Go. Does not touch effect system or type checker. Only runtime addition needed for phases 0-4.

data.title: call_with Builtin Deferred to Optional Phase 5
data.rationale: The call_with builtin is the only runtime change needed for full Option D model selection but is deferred to final optional phase. Phases 0-4 use std/ai (call) with --ai flag and model set at startup.

data.title: Bash-Only System Prompt (No Native File API)
data.rationale: All file access, editing, and execution goes through bash commands sent to env server. No native filesystem API available to LLM. System prompt provides concrete bash idioms for every common operation.

data.name: TUI Entry Point / TUI JSONL Output Mode / TypeScript TUI entry point
data.description: TypeScript entry point — CLI args, wires env server + brain + UI together

data.name: Env Server / Environment Server
data.description: Express wrapper around child_process.execSync. POST /exec, /snapshot, /restore, GET /health. Replaces Python server.

data.name: Brain Process Manager
data.description: Spawns AILANG child process, wires JSONL pipe stdin/stdout, exposes send/abort/setModel

data.name: TUI Renderer / TypeScript TUI UI renderer
data.description: pi-tui layout: scrollable history pane (Markdown + Text), status bar, command input with /model and /abort. Renders thinking, proposed_cmd, obs, done, error events.

data.name: AILANG Core RPC Loop / AILANG RPC loop module / AILANG Runtime Core / RPC Loop
data.description: The yolo brain — emit, execute, emit observation, recurse. Always yolo, no mode logic. Checks stdin for abort/model_change at top of each iteration.

data.name: AILANG Parser Module / AILANG parse module / Response Parsing
data.description: Pure functions: extract_bash, is_done, parse_cwd. Extracts bash blocks from LLM responses, detects sentinel, tracks working directory.

data.name: AILANG Prompts Module / Prompt Assembly
data.description: System prompt construction, cache hint injection, message formatting for LLM context

data.name: AILANG Environment Client
data.description: HTTP client for environment server — exec_in sends commands and returns ExecResult

data.name: Trajectory Cache
data.description: SharedMem-backed trajectory cache: get_hint for similar-task lookup, put_trajectory for storing results

data.title: Rebase-Forward Discipline (Not Cherry-Pick)
data.rationale: Move Motoko off v0.9.0 fork onto most recent AILANG release. Track upstream release tags as base, layer Motoko custom runtime extensions on top as _motoko files. Cherry-picking upstream changes backward into old base explicitly rejected.

data.title: _motoko File Naming Convention
data.rationale: Every file not existing upstream is suffixed _motoko.{go,ail,ts}. Shared-file edits are one-line additions fenced with // motoko:begin / // motoko:end comments. Module names stay upstream-compatible.

data.title: Fenced Edit Five-Line Limit and Shape Constraints
data.rationale: Each fenced block must be ≤5 lines between markers. Content must match allowed shapes: registration calls, single-line imports, dispatch forks to _motoko files, case handlers. No business logic inline, no conditionals deeper than one level.

data.title: std/ai_motoko Coexists with Upstream std/ai
data.rationale: std/ai_motoko.ail declares module std/ai_motoko (new module) rather than shadowing std/ai. Upstream std/ai remains untouched and importable. core/rpc.ail imports both as needed for streaming + Result variants.

data.title: Pin to Release Tags, Not dev HEAD
data.rationale: Target base is AILANG release tag v0.13.0 (commit 99f76ec7). Future rebases pin to release tags, not raw dev HEAD, to avoid chasing unstable intermediate commits.

data.title: dev_agent Branch Retained and Tagged (Not Deleted)
data.rationale: dev_agent remains Motoko's production branch and is not deleted, retargeted, or force-pushed until Phase 5 validation passes end-to-end on new motoko branch. Tagged pre-rebase-forward at cutover.

data.title: Parallel-Branch Safety During Migration
data.rationale: If any phase stalls or fails, Motoko keeps running on dev_agent with zero disruption. New motoko branch built from release tag in parallel.

data.title: make verify-fork-surface Enforcement Target
data.rationale: Automated Makefile target that enforces fork invariants: enumerates changed non-_motoko files, verifies fenced edits fall within motoko:begin/end blocks, checks line count ≤5, validates allowed-line shapes.

data.title: Result-Based AI Error Handling with Typed AIError
data.rationale: Re-port as internal/builtins/ai_motoko.go + std/ai_motoko.ail. AIError, AIStreamChunk, AIStreamResult types and callResult, callJsonResult, callStreamResult wrappers. HTTP non-2xx, JSON parse failure, network timeout each get right error tag.

data.title: OpenAI Streaming with SSE Parser and Abort-Correctness
data.rationale: SSE parser, chunk decoder, stream lifecycle. Ordered sequence with monotonic index. Single-budget-unit charging. Mid-stream abort emits exactly one terminal event. Error-mid-stream surfaces typed AIError.

data.title: Phase 0.5 AI Interface Spike as Kill-Switch Gate
data.rationale: Six questions (A-F) answered from v0.13.0 source before any porting begins. If streaming additivity or module coexistence can't work within fenced-edit invariants, plan revision required.

data.name: Motoko IO Builtins
data.description: _io_poll_stdin builtin — non-blocking stdin peek via bufio.Reader.Buffered(), newline-complete lines only

data.name: Motoko IO Effects
data.description: IO.pollStdin() effect op registration

data.name: OpenRouter Provider Routing
data.description: Detects openrouter/<model-id> prefix, sets base URL https://openrouter.ai/api/v1, strips prefix for upstream API call

data.name: OpenAI Streaming (SSE)
data.description: SSE parser, chunk decoder, stream lifecycle for OpenAI-compatible streaming endpoints

data.name: Motoko AI Effects
data.description: CallStream(input, onEvent) handler method, stream state, abort propagation for streaming AI calls

data.name: Motoko AI Builtins
data.description: _ai_call_result, _ai_call_json_result, _ai_call_stream_result builtins for Result-based + streaming AI

data.name: Motoko AI Stdlib Module
data.description: AIError, AIStreamChunk, AIStreamResult types; callResult, callJsonResult, callStreamResult wrappers. Coexists with upstream std/ai.

data.name: Local OpenAI Endpoint Support
data.description: openai://localhost:<port> URI parsing, custom base URL override, retryable-error classification, typed AIError surface

data.name: Fork Inventory and Rebase Playbook
data.description: Documents base tag, _motoko file inventory, fenced edits inventory, effect-set changes, rebase playbook, known risk areas, change log

data.title: Phase 0 and Phase 0.5 Only — Hard Stop at Gate
data.rationale: Execute Phase 0 (baseline, branch setup) and Phase 0.5 (AI interface spike with six gate questions). Do not start Phase 1. Report go/no-go verdict and wait for user review. Do not modify dev_agent.

data.title: Self-Contained Handoff Prompt Execution
data.rationale: Paste prompt into fresh session. Plan document is source of truth; handoff only scopes what to execute. No conversational context from planning session carried forward.

data.title: CI Regression Harness Purpose (Not GRPO, v2)
data.rationale: Build first-pass DST harness for CI regression coverage — catching behavioral drift and robustness bugs on every commit. GRPO-style trajectory-replay fidelity is explicitly a later concern.

data.title: Import-Shim Approach (No AILANG Runtime Patches)
data.rationale: Test code swaps production modules for mock modules with matching signatures via parameterized run_loop. Fork-friendly; survives upstream AILANG churn. No runtime patches.

data.title: Scripted State Machine Mock LLM (v1)
data.rationale: v1 mock LLM is scripted state machine with seeded adversarial generators. Not a hash table, not recorded tapes. Two modes: Scripted(Turn[]) for golden-path, Adversarial(seed) for fuzz.

data.title: Tier 1 Canned Environment Mock
data.rationale: Canned {stdout, stderr, exit_code} responses behind clean seam for later Tier-2 upgrade. Default miss policy is HardFail — forces agent loop to handle command failures.

data.title: AILANG-Native Test Language (No External Driver)
data.rationale: Tests live in src/core/*_properties.ail and run via ailang test. No external Go/TS driver. Property tests use AILANG_TEST_RUNS and AILANG_TEST_SEED env vars.

data.title: Parameterized run_loop for Test Injection
data.rationale: Extract main loop into function parameterized over ai_call and env_exec dependencies. Production main() becomes thin wrapper. Enables mock swapping without duplicating rpc.ail.

data.title: Fixed Seed 42 for CI Regression
data.rationale: Per-PR regression uses fixed seed 42 (or derived from commit SHA). Red stays red across reruns. Nightly fuzz uses rotating seed (github.run_id) for broader coverage.

data.title: Priority-0 Invariants: Parser Fuzzing + Loop Termination
data.rationale: Two priority-0 invariants: (a) parser fuzzing — extract_bash is total, is_done is deterministic, parse_cwd returns valid shape; (b) full-loop termination in ≤50 steps for any adversarial LLM output.

data.title: SharedMem Isolation via cache_reset() Per Property
data.rationale: If ailang test reuses one process across iterations, cache pollution makes run N+1 see run N's cache. Fallback: add cache_reset() as first line of each property body.

data.title: TUI Out of Scope for v1 DST
data.rationale: TUI frontend (src/tui/) not tested in v1 DST. Net-effect fault injection, recorded real-LLM tapes, Tier-2 in-memory FS, /abort mid-loop testing, TypeScript env-server behavior all deferred to v2+.

data.name: Parser Property Tests
data.description: Property tests for parse functions: extract_bash totality, is_done determinism, parse_cwd shape, extract_fence requirements, adversarial generators

data.name: Full-Loop Property Tests
data.description: Property tests for agent loop: termination in ≤50 steps, never panics on adversarial LLM output

data.name: Mock LLM Module
data.description: Scripted state-machine LLM mock with Scripted(Turn[]) and Adversarial(seed) modes. Matches std/ai::call signature minus Net effect.

data.name: Mock Environment Module
data.description: Tier-1 canned response env mock. Pattern-based matching, HardFail default miss policy, deterministic outputs for same fixture+command sequence.

data.name: Test Helpers
data.description: Shared test utilities: init_state, default_fixture, count_substring, naive_done_check, adversarial generators (fence, unicode_soup, huge_input, embedded_done_marker)

data.title: Colored Real-Time Thinking Trace Rendering
data.rationale: Apply dim styling to plain text in streaming thinking traces via chalk.dim; strip <thinking>/<think> tags. Route expanded think block content through same highlighting pipeline as streaming. Fix bgFn from chalk.dim to chalk.reset to preserve embedded syntax colors.

data.title: Magenta [think] Label with Dim Metadata in Headers
data.rationale: Color the [think] label with chalk.magenta for visual distinction. Keep metadata (step, chars, hint) dim. Both headerRow and bodyRow use chalk.reset as bgFn so embedded colors are not muted.

data.title: stripThinkTags Helper for XML Tag Removal
data.rationale: Dedicated helper removes <thinking>, </thinking>, <think>, </think> tags used by both streaming renderer and think block expansion. Structural noise removed before rendering.

data.title: Color Rendering for Streamed JSON Blocks
data.rationale: Streamed fenced JSON blocks are color rendered during streaming. Partial/incomplete JSON renders in stable highlighted form via tolerant tokenizer. Non-tool JSON always visible. Tool-envelope JSON collapsed when planned-tools panel active.

data.title: Tool-Envelope Visibility Policy with Confidence Levels
data.rationale: Three confidence levels: confident_strict (JSON parse succeeds + tool_calls array), confident_heuristic (partial detection of tool_calls/tool/id tokens), not_confident. Hidden with placeholder [tool json hidden; see Planned Tools]. Deterministic precedence — no retroactive flip-flop.

data.title: Tolerant JSON Tokenizer for Partial/Incomplete Input
data.rationale: Dedicated json-highlight.ts module with tolerant tokenizer for keys, strings, numbers, booleans, null, punctuation. Works on incomplete buffers without throwing. Line-based output for TTY rendering.

data.title: MOTOKO_SHOW_TOOL_JSON_STREAM Debug Override
data.rationale: Env var MOTOKO_SHOW_TOOL_JSON_STREAM=1 forces tool JSON blocks to render highlighted in stream area alongside planned-tools panel. Non-TTY plain mode stays compact.

data.name: JSON Token Highlighter
data.description: Tolerant incremental-safe JSON token highlighter. Line-based highlight function for UI reuse. Handles partial/incomplete buffers without throwing.

data.title: Incremental Segment Model for Live Markdown Streaming
data.rationale: Three segment types: plain (text outside fences), code_complete (closed fenced block), code_open (open/incomplete fence). Segmenter consumes thinking_delta incrementally and emits updated segment list per stream ID. Deterministic segment keys for diff updates.

data.title: 50ms Render Throttle with Memory Caps and Segment Diffing
data.rationale: Render throttle at 50ms per stream. Segment diffing updates only changed rows. Memory caps: 128 KiB parser buffer, 12 KiB visible live render per stream. Segment-aware trimming drops oldest complete segments first. Truncated streams finalize at stream end.

data.title: Fixed Stream-Block Anchor for Scroll Stability
data.rationale: Never insert/remove rows above existing non-stream history. Keep fixed stream block anchor per stream ID, mutate only rows within that block. Avoid replacing stream rows with growing single text blob.

data.title: Final Canonical Markdown Render from Full Payload (Not Capped Buffer)
data.rationale: Final canonical markdown render uses the full assistant final payload, not the capped live rawBuffer. On stream end, collapse transient rows and append one final Markdown render. For aborted/errored, flush pending updates, preserve partial snapshot.

data.name: Stream Markdown Segmenter
data.description: Incremental fence detector for markdown stream buffers. Emits deterministic segment keys and truncation metadata. Supports language tags. Segment-aware trimming.

data.title: Two-Layer Rendering with Single Tool Execution Timeline
data.rationale: Derive incremental tool plan view from streamed text. Render dedicated Planned Tools (streaming) block expanded by default in TTY. When runtime emits execution events, map by canonical identity key: planned → running → done/error. One stable row per tool.

data.title: Canonical Identity Keys for Tool Deduplication
data.rationale: Prefer step + request_id + tool_call_id. Fallback: step + request_id + tool + normalized_args_hash + first_seen_index. Never dedupe globally by tool_call_id alone. Reconciliation states: planned_unexecuted, runtime_only, filtered. Runtime result always wins terminal state.

data.title: 128 KiB Stream Cap with 64 KiB Parser Window
data.rationale: streamText cap: 128 KiB per assistant step. Parser candidate window: 64 KiB rolling tail. On overflow, drop oldest bytes and set stream_truncated_for_parse flag. Parse only when candidate structurally complete; ignore partial fragments.

data.name: Tool Plan Parser
data.description: Tolerant incremental extractor over streamed deltas. Detects candidate fenced JSON or bare JSON containing tool_calls. Publishes normalized planned calls when syntactically complete.

data.title: Local Token Estimate via chars÷4 (Not Provider-Reported)
data.rationale: Show context window usage as local estimate: (total_chars + 3) / 4 across composed system prompt + message history. ±20% accuracy. Good enough for 'am I about to run out?' without touching vendored ailang/ fork or per-provider adapters.

data.title: Per-Model Context Window Limits in Runtime
data.rationale: Runtime owns per-model context-window table via context_limit_for(). TUI just renders what it's told. openrouter/ prefix stripped to recurse into known models. Unknown models return 0 → hide ratio. Known: Claude 200K, GPT-4o 128K, Gemini Flash 1M, Gemini Pro 2M.

data.title: Color Escalation at 75% and 90% Thresholds
data.rationale: Yellow at ≥75% context usage, red at ≥90%. ctx segment assembled separately from status bar to avoid chalk nesting issues. When limit === 0, render only token count without ratio.

data.name: Context Usage Estimator
data.description: Pure AILANG module: estimate_tokens(msgs, system) and context_limit_for(model). Char-count based token estimation. Per-model limit lookup with openrouter/ prefix stripping.

data.title: JSONL Session Logging to logfile/ Directory
data.rationale: SessionLogger class creates timestamped JSONL files (session_<ISO>.jsonl) under logfile/. One JSON object per line matches runtime protocol. Wired into event pipeline in index.ts. Per-invocation file: new SessionLogger each spawnRuntimeProcess call.

data.name: Session Logger
data.description: Creates logfile/ directory, opens write stream to timestamped JSONL file. log(event: AgentEvent) writes one JSON line. close() flushes and closes stream.

data.title: Hybrid-Only Tool Rendering Model with Renderer Architecture
data.rationale: Implement new hybrid-only tool rendering model without changing runtime protocol shapes. Renderer architecture with fallback, shared status-line formatting, expansion/collapse for tool details, grouped ReadFile rendering. No protocol changes.

data.title: Ctrl+O Global Toggle for Tool Detail Expansion
data.rationale: Global toggle for tool detail expansion. Expanded detail rows show stdout up to 8 lines, stderr up to 4 lines, hidden-line messaging. Think-block controls (Ctrl+T) unchanged. Line-width truncation tied to terminal width.

data.title: 50ms Progress Coalescing with Terminal Bypass
data.rationale: Coalesced row refresh for progress-heavy updates. Per-row last render timestamp cache. 50ms coalescing skipped for terminal transitions (done/failed) and batch finalization. Raw data always stored; only visual refresh throttled.

data.title: Grouped ReadFile Rendering for 2+ Calls
data.rationale: Group rendering for 2+ ReadFile calls in same request_id. Group header + child entries with per-child status. Single-row rendering preserved for one ReadFile call. Progress + finalization update group header deterministically.

data.title: File-Edit Diff Rendering with Reused LLM Diff Helpers
data.rationale: Dedicated renderer for WriteFile/file-edit tool family. Collapsed hunk summary + expandable bounded diff body. Reuse existing LLM diff rendering helpers from TUI_Code_Block_Rendering via adapter layer. Fallback safe when structured diff absent.

data.title: Edge-Case Hardening: Missing IDs, Out-of-Order, Dupes
data.rationale: Synthetic ID generation for missing request_id/tool_call_id. Lazy header/placeholder row creation when results arrive before calls. Unseen-row finalization marker for delegated done mismatches. No stranded [running] rows at completion.

data.name: Tool Render Types
data.description: ToolRowStatus, ToolRowDetails, ToolRenderer, ToolRenderCtx type definitions for tool call rendering architecture

data.name: Tool Render Formatters
data.description: Shared status-line formatting helpers: icon/title/description/badge/meta field model. Header and row compact formatting.

data.name: Tool Renderers
data.description: Renderer registry and generic fallback path. Per-tool-type renderer implementations. Exception-safe fallback rendering.

data.name: Tool Grouping
data.description: Group-state model and group keying for ReadFile batch detection. Group header + child entry rendering.

data.title: Language-Aware Syntax Highlighting via highlightCode Hook
data.rationale: Add highlightCode function in ui.ts wired into MINIMAL_THEME. Language routing by fence tag: TypeScript/JS, Python, AILANG, Shell, Diff. Lightweight in-file lexer — no external package. Fallback to chalk.dim for unknown languages.

data.title: Exact AILANG 41-Keyword Reserved Set from Language Reference
data.rationale: AILANG highlighter uses exact reserved keywords from language reference: if/then/else/match/with, func/pure/let/letrec/in, type/class/instance/forall/exists/deriving, module/import/export/extern/as, test/tests/property/properties/assert, requires/ensures/invariant, spawn/parallel/channel/send/recv, true/false/and/or/not. Canonical set size exactly 41.

data.title: Diff/Patch Rendering with Deterministic Precedence
data.rationale: Explicit diff/patch routing in highlightCodeLines. File headers, hunk headers, additions, deletions, context lines with distinct styling. Ordered matching with dedicated tests for ambiguity guard (--- as file header, not generic deletion).

data.title: GitHub-Style Diff Rendering with Background Colors
data.rationale: Opt-in mode via TUI_DIFF_STYLE=github. Green background for additions, red for deletions. Language-aware inner highlighting after prefix marker. ANSI composition helper preserves background through token-level foreground resets.

data.title: Runtime-Generated Banner Scaled to Terminal Width
data.rationale: Replace fixed-size pre-generated ASCII header with runtime-generated banner. Width from process.stdout.columns, clamped to safe range. Block-character + ANSI truecolor style preserved. No shelling out at startup.

data.title: Preprocessed Pixel Data Asset (Not PNG Decode at Runtime)
data.rationale: One-time generation script converts gits2.png into compact TS/JSON pixel data committed as banner-pixels.ts. Runtime renderer reads this asset directly. Avoids adding jimp or other heavy image decode dependencies to startup.

data.title: Deprecate Static banner.ts Generation
data.rationale: Repurpose misc/gen-banner.mjs to generate preprocessed pixel asset. Explicitly deprecate src/tui/src/banner.ts generation. Remove dead references.

data.name: Runtime Banner Generator
data.description: renderBanner(opts): string[] and computeBannerWidth(columns?). Width clamping, block-character + ANSI truecolor rendering from preprocessed pixel data.

data.title: Run State Enum: idle, thinking, tools_wait, tools_run, done, error
data.rationale: Explicit UI state machine: session_start → idle, thinking stream → thinking, tool_calls → tools_wait, tool_results running → tools_run, done → done, error → error. Status bar always displays one explicit state. Color accents: blue thinking, yellow tools, green done, red error.

data.title: Status Spinner + Elapsed Timer During Active States
data.rationale: Active spinner in thinking, tools_wait, tools_run states. Timer resets on state transitions. Visible progress every 100-250ms while waiting. Last-update indicator: 'last update: Ns ago'.

data.title: Completion Handoff Message on Task Done
data.rationale: On done, append: 'Task complete. You can now type a follow-up.' Users know exactly when follow-ups are accepted. Mid-run input locked with 'Input locked: task still running. Use /abort to stop.'

data.title: Delegated Tool Batch Counters with Progress Events
data.rationale: Track {total, running, done, failed} per request. tool_results phase extended to running/progress/done. Partial progress emitted after each tool finishes. Status shows tools: done/total and failed count while batch active.

data.title: Phase Transition Markers in History
data.rationale: Explicit markers: 'Brain is reasoning...', 'Waiting for delegated tool results...', 'Tool results received. Continuing reasoning...' Delay ownership is clear (brain vs tools). Slow-operation hints at ≥10s thinking / ≥20s tools.

data.title: Declarative SlashCommand[] Registry Replacing Ad-Hoc Switch
data.rationale: Adopt oh-my-pi pattern: declarative SlashCommand[] with name, description, subcommands, execute, getArgumentCompletions, getInlineHint. Replace ad-hoc handleCommand switch. Zero coupling to om session/auth stack. parseSlashCommand splits /model args → {cmd, args}.

data.title: Replace Input with Editor for Slash-Command Autocomplete
data.rationale: Editor from pi-tui supports setAutocompleteProvider() natively. Tab-complete /model, /abort, model names. Fuzzy matching. Surgical swap within existing 1-2 day Phase 2c budget.

data.name: Slash Command Registry
data.description: Declarative SlashCommand[] registry: BUILTIN_SLASH_COMMANDS, parseSlashCommand, createAutocompleteProvider. Model argument completions. Command execution dispatches to Brain/UI.

data.title: Generic Tool Contract in Core: ToolCallEnvelope and ToolResultEnvelope
data.rationale: Core-owned envelopes only: ToolCallEnvelope = {id, tool, arguments: Json}, ToolResultEnvelope = {tool_call_id, tool, exit_code, stdout, stderr, metadata: Json}. Extension-specific aliasing, coercion, policy, and handler semantics move out of core.

data.title: Defer Descriptor/Data-Driven Registration to Follow-Up Plan
data.rationale: Keep extension registration enum-based during disentangling. Scope control: disentangling already spans parser, RPC/telemetry, dispatch, runtime backend, type boundaries. Descriptor migration would simultaneously alter registry model, startup, dispatch.

data.title: Eight-Phase Migration with Dual-Path Compatibility Window
data.rationale: Phase 0 behavior freeze, Phase 1 parallel generic contracts, Phase 2 dual parser path, Phase 3 generic dispatch, Phase 4 Omnigraph normalization extraction, Phase 5 generic telemetry, Phase 6 tool_runtime cleanup, Phase 7 remove Omnigraph from core ADTs, Phase 8 retire compatibility shims.

data.name: Generic Tool Contract
data.description: Defines ToolCallEnvelope and ToolResultEnvelope — core-owned generic tool call contract. Extension-agnostic envelope types for parser, dispatch, and telemetry.

data.name: Omnigraph Normalization
data.description: Omnigraph alias map and coercion rules moved from core parser. Takes generic call envelopes, produces executable Omnigraph requests.

data.title: First-Class Extension System for Core with Hook Dispatch
data.rationale: Design reusable extension substrate supporting Semi-Formal and future capabilities. Hooks: on_build_system_prompt (pure), on_budget_plan (pure), on_tool_call (pure), on_solver_candidate (effectful). Deterministic extension order with deny-wins conflict resolution.

data.title: Kernel-Enforced Hard Limits Non-Overridable
data.rationale: Kernel always enforces: global max steps, per-phase clamping, forbidden tool classes in read-only mode, finalization validator pass, abort/exit semantics. Extensions cannot override these.

data.title: Separate Pure and Effectful Hook Registries
data.rationale: Pure hooks (prompt, budget, tool policy) and effectful hooks (on_solver_candidate) in separate registry shapes. Avoids forcing prompt-only extensions to satisfy maximal effect signatures.

data.title: Env-Driven Extension Configuration (Phase 1)
data.rationale: CORE_EXT_ORDER=semi_formal,... controls extension list and order. CORE_EXT_STRICT controls fail-fast on hook errors. CORE_EXT_HOOK_TIMEOUT_MS for effectful hook timeout. Phase 2 adds file-driven config.

data.title: Test Dummy Extension for Substrate Validation
data.rationale: Configurable test_dummy extension controlled by env vars (EXT_DUMMY_PROMPT, EXT_DUMMY_TOOL_DECISION, EXT_DUMMY_FINALIZE, EXT_DUMMY_BUDGET_TOTAL) validates every hook dispatch path, conflict resolution, and fallback before real extensions are built.

data.name: Extension Type Definitions
data.description: ExtCtx, ConfigPatch, PromptPatch, BudgetPatch, ToolDecision, FinalizeDecision, ExtensionEvent types. Core data model for extension hooks.

data.name: Extension Registry
data.description: Extension registration, parse_core_ext_order, resolve by name. Deterministic ordering. Activation via CORE_EXT_ORDER env var.

data.name: Extension Runtime Dispatcher
data.description: Hook dispatch: fold_prompt_pure, decide_one_policy, dispatch_handle_from, dispatch_intercept_from, decide_one_finalize. Conflict resolution.

data.name: Test Dummy Extension
data.description: Configurable extension for substrate validation. Env-var-controlled behavior exercises every hook dispatch path. Emits dummy_hook events for test assertions.

data.title: Extension-First Compose Dispatch with Rollout Gate
data.rationale: Move compose tool handling from rpc.ail into compose extension handler path. Temporary rollout gate CORE_COMPOSE_EXTENSION_ONLY (0=fallback, 1=extension-only) with rollback playbook. Compose calls flow through dispatch_tool_handle only.

data.title: Compose Mode Gating Moved to Extension on_tool_policy
data.rationale: Move composition mode gating (inline/off/subagent) into compose extension on_tool_policy. Core keeps generic Allow/Deny semantics. Env var AILANG_COMPOSITION_MODE read inside compose extension only.

data.title: Remove Compose Constructor from Core ToolCallReq ADT
data.rationale: Remove Compose from ToolCallReq in types.ail. Compose exists only as extension envelope tool. Core ADTs for native tools only: ReadFile, Search, WriteFile, EditFile, BashExec, RunTests.

data.title: Core Prompt Templates Remain Extension-Neutral
data.rationale: Remove compose-specific behavioral instructions from core prompt templates. Compose guidance added via extension prompt patch only. User-facing observation contract preserved: summary, attempts, validator outcome.

data.title: CI Allowlist-Based Boundary Checks for Compose
data.rationale: Forbidden in core paths: Compose( constructor matches, compose-specific helper symbols after Phase 6, compose-specific mode branching after Phase 2. Allowed only in compose extension modules and allowlisted tests/docs.

data.title: Replace PureExt/EffectExt ADTs with ExtensionHooks Record
data.rationale: Single ExtensionHooks record with typed fields replaces sealed ADT dispatch. Adding extension = constructing record + appending to list. Function-typed record fields work (spike-confirmed). Union effect row on effectful hooks — per-extension ceilings via [effects].max at publish time.

data.title: Each Extension Exports register() -> ExtensionHooks
data.rationale: Each extension (test_dummy, compose, omnigraph) exports register() function that closes over config/prompts. registry.ail becomes string->register() lookup table. Phase 2 lifts to pkg/ imports with ailang.toml manifests.

data.title: Fold Over [ExtensionHooks] List Instead of Per-Extension Match Trees
data.rationale: Every match tree collapses to ext.on_build_system_prompt(ctx) etc. Seven dispatch helpers lose their per-extension match arms. Grep for extension names in runtime.ail/types.ail must return zero hits after Phase 1.

data.title: TestDummy Telemetry Moved from Hook Body to Dispatcher-Side Wrapper
data.rationale: on_tool_policy/on_build_system_prompt/on_budget_plan declared pure on record. TestDummy's emit_dummy_hook (needs now() + println) moves to dispatcher-side wrapper that logs after pure hook returns. Same events at same sequence points.

data.title: Phase 2: Lift Extensions to Packages via ailang.toml
data.rationale: Extensions live at pkg/sunholo/motoko-<name>. module_prefix preserves existing paths. [effects].max per package acts as publish-time lint. AGENT.md per package for prompt text. ailang publish --dry-run validates effect ceilings and contracts.

data.title: Generic MCP Layer as Library (Not Registered Extension)
data.rationale: Generic MCP client layer (src/core/ext/mcp/) is a library provider extensions import — no ExtensionHooks, no registry entry, no AGENT.md. Provider extensions (exa_search) own their config, tool mappings, and hooks.

data.title: Node.js MCP Bridge Script via Shell Exec
data.rationale: Generic scripts/mcp-call.mjs (~150 lines, zero npm deps) handles JSON-RPC 2.0 POST, SSE parsing, auth via env var (never CLI arg). AILANG side calls via exec wrapper. API keys stay in env vars. First SSE data: line wins.

data.title: Extract compress_output to motoko_core Shared Module
data.rationale: Move compress_output from context_mode/compress.ail to src/core/compress.ail in motoko_core. Both context_mode and mcp extensions import from shared location. Avoids wrong dependency direction and code drift.

data.name: MCP Types
data.description: McpServerConfig, McpToolMapping types. base_url, auth_env_var, auth_style, tool_filter, timeout_ms, max_output_chars.

data.name: MCP Exec Wrapper
data.description: bridge_args construction, run_mcp_tool (shell out to mcp-call.mjs, parse output, return ToolResultEnvelope), list_mcp_tools. Timeout via timeout command. Compress output before returning.

data.name: MCP Tool Name Resolution
data.description: resolve_tool(raw, mappings), all_tool_names. Eliminates repetitive canonical_tool_name if/else chains. Matches canonical + all aliases.

data.name: MCP Bridge Script
data.description: Node.js script: JSON-RPC 2.0 POST, SSE parsing, auth via env var. Outputs single JSON line to stdout. Error-safe (exit 0 on MCP errors).

data.title: Context-Mode as Shell Exec (Not HTTP Sidecar)
data.rationale: Extension shells out to context-mode CLI via std/process.exec. Matches omnigraph pattern. No Net effect, no sidecar lifecycle, SQLite persists across invocations. Fallback: Delegate on NotFound/SpawnFailed.

data.title: Lazy Session Snapshot Loaded on First Tool Call
data.rationale: register() cannot shell out (no Process effect). Session snapshot loaded on first on_tool_handle call, cached in SharedMem for subsequent on_build_system_prompt. Static routing instructions from AGENT.md via readFile during register().

data.title: Nine Context-Mode Tools Routed Through Extension
data.rationale: Provided tools: ctx_execute, ctx_batch_execute, ctx_execute_file, ctx_index, ctx_search, ctx_fetch_and_index, ctx_stats, ctx_doctor, ctx_purge. on_tool_handle dispatches to context-mode CLI. Tool name normalization (PascalCase, snake_case, dot-notation).

data.title: Prompt-Based Curl/Wget Blocking (Not Hard Enforcement)
data.rationale: on_tool_policy receives ToolCallEnvelope for named tools only — bash commands are HTTP POST via env_client. Curl/wget blocking via system prompt routing instructions. Hard enforcement deferred to future on_before_bash_exec hook.

data.title: Output Compression in on_tool_handle (Not on_response_intercept)
data.rationale: on_response_intercept sees raw LLM response text, not tool output. InterceptHandled replaces entire response processing — would swallow normal tool-call responses. Compression in on_tool_handle when building ToolResultEnvelope and in exec.ail wrapper.

data.name: Context-Mode Extension
data.description: register() entry point, tool dispatch, on_tool_handle routing to context-mode CLI. Nine ctx_* tools. Fallback Delegate on binary not found.

data.name: Context-Mode Exec Wrapper
data.description: Shell exec wrapper for context-mode CLI. run_context_mode function. Output compression before ToolResultEnvelope.

data.name: Context-Mode Prompt Module
data.description: System prompt routing instructions. AGENT.md loader. Session snapshot caching in SharedMem.

data.name: Context-Mode Compression
data.description: Pure output compression: strip ANSI, collapse whitespace, truncate repetitive output. compress_output(text, max_chars).

data.title: Thin Exa Extension Using Generic MCP Layer (Zero Protocol Logic)
data.rationale: exa_search extension contains zero protocol logic — all MCP handling in generic layer. Provides: Exa server URL, API key env var, tool name mappings (4 canonical → MCP names), AGENT.md routing card. ~80-100 lines of AILANG.

data.title: Four Exa Tool Mappings: Search, DeepSearch, SearchCode, Crawl
data.rationale: Canonical names mapped to MCP: ExaSearch→web_search_exa, ExaSearchDeep→deep_search_exa, ExaSearchCode→get_code_context_exa, ExaCrawl→crawling_exa. Each with dot-notation aliases.

data.name: Exa Search Extension
data.description: register() function. Exa-specific config (base_url, auth_env_var, tool_filter). Four tool mappings. Hooks: prompt, tool_handle (calls run_mcp_tool), no-op on policy/intercept/finalize.

data.name: Exa Search Routing Card
data.description: LLM routing instructions: use ExaSearch for general searches, ExaSearchDeep for NL queries, ExaSearchCode for code, ExaCrawl for URL extraction. Extension tools are authoritative.

data.title: CLI-Only Omnigraph Extension (No HTTP Server)
data.rationale: Port Pi-targeted Omnigraph PoC into Motoko extension using shell exec pattern. Four typed tools: omnigraph_read, omnigraph_mutate, omnigraph_branch, omnigraph_status. CLI is authoritative — no HTTP client.

data.title: Main-Branch Write Guardrail in on_tool_policy
data.rationale: OmnigraphMutate with branch=main → Deny('refuses to write to main directly; create a feature branch first'). Cleaner separation from handler. BashExec bypass documented as acceptable residual.

data.title: AGENT_PROMPT.md Injected via on_build_system_prompt
data.rationale: Extension reads omnigraph/AGENT_PROMPT.md via readFile at init. Cached on ExtRuntime.omnigraph_prompt. Injected as PromptPatch.append on every turn. File absent → empty patch silently.

data.title: No Positional repo.omni URI in CLI Args (Pi Extension Bug Avoided)
data.rationale: Pi extension shipped bug passing positional repo.omni. AILANG port never passes positional URI or --uri — omnigraph.yaml resolves URI uniformly for all subcommands. Documented as comment in exec.ail.

data.title: Phase 0 Toolchain Install: Rust + Omnigraph from Source
data.rationale: No arm64 Linux binary exists. Build from source via cargo build --release --locked. Persistent /opt/omnigraph-src + ~/.cargo survive restarts. scripts/install-prerequisites.sh --with-omnigraph idempotent.

data.name: Omnigraph Extension Entry
data.description: Hook bodies: on_build_system_prompt (AGENT_PROMPT.md injection), on_tool_policy (main-branch guardrail), on_tool_handle (dispatch to exec.run_omnigraph).

data.name: Omnigraph Exec Wrapper
data.description: run_omnigraph(args, cwd, timeout_ms) via std/process.exec. parseOutput rule: try JSON decode, wrap raw on failure. No positional URI in arg arrays.

data.name: Omnigraph Tool Types
data.description: OmnigraphReadReq, OmnigraphMutateReq, OmnigraphBranchReq, OmnigraphStatusReq request types. Mirror TypeBox shapes from Pi extension.

data.name: Omnigraph Guardrail / Omnigraph Guardrails
data.description: Main-branch detector for on_tool_policy. is_main_branch_mutation predicate.

data.name: Omnigraph Prompt Module
data.description: AGENT_PROMPT.md reader via load_agent_prompt. PromptPatch builder. Cached on ExtRuntime at init.

data.title: Reshape X1 Substrate: Add on_tool_handle + on_response_intercept
data.rationale: Grow X1 from policy hooks to capability hooks. Rename on_tool_call→on_tool_policy. Add on_tool_handle (provided_tools, first-match-wins). Add on_response_intercept (fires before tool-call parse, first-intercept-wins). Enrich ExtCtx with workdir, env_server_url, history_slice, state_key.

data.title: Defensive Hook-Error Isolation with ext_error Events
data.rationale: Dispatcher wraps every hook invocation in try-equivalent. On error: emit ext_error event, treat contribution as NoOpinion/NoIntercept/Delegate/NoDecision. Runtime continues unaffected. Test with deliberately-crashing dummy hook.

data.title: Clean Cutover: Delete /compose HTTP Endpoint + TS SF Guards
data.rationale: Two cutover points: Phase 2.6 deletes /compose and /exec-ailang HTTP routes. Phase 4 deletes TS SF guard code and compose-claimcheck.ts. Tag commits before each for clean reset. No legacy path kept behind flag.

data.title: Port Existing TS Tests as Golden Behavioral Tests
data.rationale: Jest-tested surface promoted to golden behavioral tests running via thin TS shim that invokes AILANG extension. Cross-language equivalence checked at boundary. Golden fixtures frozen before Phase 1 porting.

data.title: Faithful Translation First, Refactor Later
data.rationale: Porting directive: reproduce source logic 1:1, keep variable names adjacent, no opportunistic cleanup. Refactoring in separate pass after golden tests green. Prevents silent behavioral changes from 'clean up while porting'.

data.title: Regex Phase 5 Default: Go-Builtin (Option B) if Triggered
data.rationale: If LinesRegex pattern outside lowered subset blocks real task, implement via Go's regexp as builtins (Option B). ~60 LOC Go + ~20 LOC AILANG wrapper. Option A (pure AILANG NFA) only if dogfooding/language showcase is the driver.

data.name: Compose Extension Entry
data.description: Hook registrations, provided_tools=['compose']. Author loop integration, snippet execution via std/process.exec, event emission.

data.name: Compose SF2 Guard
data.description: Effect-set witness + marker blacklist. parseDeclaredEffects, composeSnippetGuard. Z3 contracts on pure predicates.

data.name: Compose SF3 Validator
data.description: Certificate parser, PREMISES/TRACE/CONCLUSION validator. Output contracts: NonEmpty, ContainsAll, LinesRegex (lowered subset), Certificate.

data.name: Compose SF5 ClaimCheck
data.description: Round-trip informalization: pass 1 informalizer (no intent), pass 2 comparator (no certificate). Separation invariant enforced. Verdict JSON parsing + repair.

data.name: Compose Retry Module
data.description: Attempt-loop state machine, prior-errors accumulator, hint selection by error category.

data.name: Compose Telemetry
data.description: Record shape + accumulators. Mirror field names currently emitted by env-server for TUI compatibility.

data.title: SF2: Effect-Set Evidence Witness Replaces Substring Heuristic
data.rationale: Replace readFile(/exec( substring check with declared effect set inspection. AILANG type system validates effects — FS covers readFile, Process covers exec. Marker blacklist narrowed (drop assume/assumption). Env var AILANG_COMPOSE_EFFECT_GUARD=1 on by default.

data.title: SF1: Certificate Template in Author Prompt
data.rationale: Teach subagent to structure stdout as certificate with explicit PREMISES, TRACE, CONCLUSION. intent_kind field: analyze|list|transform|compute|fetch|summarize. Different template per kind. Unifies with SF2: isAnalysisIntent switches to intent_kind field.

data.title: SF3: Certificate Validator for expected_output.kind=certificate
data.rationale: Extend Phase 3b validator to parse certificate structure. Count premise lines (min_premises), validate path → arrow → text shape, require non-empty TRACE/CONCLUSION. ASCII -> and Unicode → both accepted. Failure → exit_code=2 retry.

data.title: SF4: Citation-Binding Check (Every Premise Path Was Actually Read)
data.rationale: Static pre-filter: parse premise paths, parse snippet source for literal readFile/listDir/exec arguments. Prefix-match acceptance (listDir+entry). Strict mode requires per-file literals. Catches cheapest fabrication before SF5 LLM calls.

data.title: SF5: Round-Trip Informalization (ClaimCheck Two-Pass)
data.rationale: Two separate LLM calls: pass 1 informalizer describes certificate without seeing intent, pass 2 comparator compares that description against original intent without seeing certificate. Catches 'technically true but off-target' failures. 96.3% vs 69.4% accuracy empirically.

data.name: Compose ClaimCheck Orchestrator
data.description: SF5 two-pass orchestration: spawn subprocesses for informalizer and comparator, event bridging, verdict parsing. Separation invariant enforcement.

data.title: Read-Only Tool Whitelist for Compose Author Pass
data.rationale: Five tools: read_file, grep, list_dir, file_exists, stat. All pure reads. All paths sandboxed. Path denylist blocks secrets (.env*, *.key, .git/**, etc.). Executed inside extension host. Results feed premise ledger.

data.title: tool_call Fence + JSON Protocol (Not Native Tool-Calling API)
data.rationale: One action per turn. Author emits tool_call fence with JSON request. Extension parses, dispatches, returns result as observation. Provider-neutral, reuses fence-parsing pattern, fully inspectable text transcript.

data.title: Premise Ledger Binding in SF3: Three-Field Grammar
data.rationale: PREMISES lines use <path> | "<verbatim_quote>" | <paraphrase> (pipe-delimited). Ledger-bound: quote is verbatim substring of ledger entry. Snippet-bound: path cited via snippet effect. Unbound: fails validation. Malformed: fails with format hint.

data.title: SF2 Accepts Effectless Snippets with Ledger-Bound Premises
data.rationale: With author tools, snippet may declare no effects because reads happened at author time. Update SF2: effectless snippet passes for analyze/summarize iff certificate contains ≥1 ledger-bound premise. telemetry: sf2_witness_source = {declared_effects|author_ledger|both}.

data.name: Compose Author Tools
data.description: Whitelist dispatcher, sandbox enforcement, path denylist. Per-tool implementations over std/fs and std/process.exec. grep glob expansion, fallback AILANG scanner.

data.name: Compose Author Loop
data.description: Multi-turn state machine: generate→parse→dispatch→feed back. Failure modes: budget exhaustion, malformed JSON, no-action. tool_call and ailang fence detection.

data.name: Compose Premise Ledger
data.description: PremiseLedger type: entries accumulator, query-by-path, query-by-substring. Pure, Z3 contracts on append-only monotonicity. Survives retries (history resets, ledger persists).

data.title: Option 2 Structured Tool-Call Authoring (Skeleton + Free-Text Bodies)
data.rationale: Replace free-text AILANG emission with structured tool-call surface. Model invokes set_module, add_import, define_func etc; dispatcher renders canonical AILANG. Function bodies remain free-text. Specifically targets Gemma 4 parse-error rate near zero. Rides existing fenced tool_call JSON protocol.

data.title: Dispatcher Owns AILANG Shape; Model Owns Content
data.rationale: Syntax and effect-row well-formedness become dispatcher invariants. Model chooses content via structured tool calls. Schema validation, reference integrity, import/symbol validation, effect-row hinting (best-effort), scope checking, canonical rendering all in dispatcher. ailang check at finalize is authoritative.

data.title: Structured-Authoring Fallback After N Consecutive Finalize Failures
data.rationale: AILANG_COMPOSE_STRUCTURED_AUTHORING_FALLBACK_AFTER=3. Remain structured-first until N consecutive failures or budget exhaustion, then fall back to free-text authoring. Fallback=0 disables and terminates with structured-authoring failure.

data.name: Structured Authoring Dispatcher
data.description: Dispatches surface A/E authoring tools. Schema validation, reference integrity, import/symbol validation, effect-row hinting, scope checking. Atomic per-call state mutations.

data.name: Canonical AILANG Renderer
data.description: Pretty-prints internal program state to canonical AILANG source. Guarantees {/; vs =/in discipline and import ordering.

data.name: Program State Accumulator
data.description: Internal program AST and accumulator state for structured authoring. Tracks module, imports, types, functions, main.

data.title: Provider-Native Tool Calling as Structured Authoring Transport
data.rationale: Same dispatcher interface as structured authoring but driven by provider-native tool-call events (OpenAI Responses API, Anthropic tool_use, Gemini function calls). Adapter layer normalizes provider shapes. Quality optimization on top of fence pipeline — not a prerequisite.

data.title: Unified Tool Semantics with Two Execution Backends (Native + Delegated)
data.rationale: Brain owns tool model, parsing, batching, IDs, schemas, warnings, observation shaping, step control. Native backend for what AILANG supports well (ReadFile, Search, WriteFile, synchronous BashExec). Delegated backend for streaming subprocesses, per-call cwd, hard-cancelable processes.

data.title: Deterministic Backend Selection per Tool Call
data.rationale: backend_for(call) pure function classifies each tool call as Native or Delegated. Semantic file tools native; streaming/stderr/cwd/cancel needs delegated. Shell metacharacters delegate. Model expresses intent; brain routes.

data.title: Unified Result Model Regardless of Execution Backend
data.rationale: Results normalized into same ADT family for native and delegated execution. Brain owns result normalization. LLM should not care whether result came from native or delegated. ToolResultMeta carries truncation, byte counts, SHA-256 hashes.

data.title: Brain-Owned Tool Execution for Stronger AILANG Thesis
data.rationale: Move tool execution into AILANG brain using native process/stream primitives. Brain becomes system of record for tool execution. TUI becomes thinner: spawn brain, render events, forward abort/model_change. env-server.ts removed from normal execution path.

data.title: Mandatory Correlation IDs on Every Tool Call
data.rationale: Every tool call has stable id. Every chunk, status event, final result references that id. Enables traceability, robust matching independent of array order, future retry/partial re-execution.

data.title: Bounded Output with Explicit Truncation Metadata
data.rationale: Per-call caps: stdout 16KB model cap, stderr 8KB. UI chunk cap 2KB per event. Keep head for stdout, tail for stderr. Stable SHA-256 hashes of full output when clipped. Prevents context window destruction from cat/grep.

data.title: Machine-Readable Tool Schema in System Prompt
data.rationale: Project-local JSON schema contract describing tool JSON to model. Required fields per tool, correlation IDs, batching rules. Not provider-native tool calling — project-local contract.

data.title: Hot-Swap Brain Architecture with Conversation Continuity
data.rationale: TypeScript UI never restarts. AILANG brain subprocess killed and respawned with new code. Full conversation history preserved across swap via RESUME_MSGS_FILE. state_snapshot + upgrade_ready JSONL events.

data.title: Atomic Filesystem Swap via rename(2)
data.rationale: Old brain killed before filesystem changes. rename(swe, swe_backup) then rename(swe_next, swe) — atomic on same filesystem. swe_next always present as recovery target.

data.name: Upgrade Manager
data.description: Manages brain upgrade lifecycle: state_snapshot capture, upgrade_ready validation, test suite execution, probe brain test, atomic filesystem swap, rollback.

data.title: Mandatory End-to-End Smoke Test (smoke_main) Before Swap
data.rationale: Candidate brain must pass smoke_main (exercises /exec path, emits session_start→thinking→proposed_cmd→obs→done) plus probe_main (protocol startup). Not just type-check. Prevents false positives from brains that compile but can't execute.

data.title: Transactional Swap with Watchdog Timer and Auto-Rollback
data.rationale: Watchdog timer (SWAP_WATCHDOG_MS default 15s). If resumed brain fails to emit session_start before timeout, auto-rollback: kill failed brain, rename swe_failed, restore swe_backup, spawn old brain from snapshot. UI never permanently blocked.

data.title: Strict Path Allowlist for upgrade_ready
data.rationale: Frontend treats upgrade_ready as untrusted input. Candidate new_swe_dir must be exactly ${AGENT_DIR}/swe_next. Reject symlinks, non-existent paths, non-directories. Required files must exist: rpc.ail, types.ail, parse.ail, prompts.ail, env_client.ail, cache.ail, agents_md.ail.

data.title: Isolated Candidate Validation in Canonical swe/ Root
data.rationale: Build isolated workspace at .agent/upgrade-work/<id>/. Copy candidate into canonical swe/ path. No --relax-modules. Strict ailang check on every module. Avoids false positives from module path mismatches.

data.title: One JSONL Round-Trip Per Step (Non-Negotiable)
data.rationale: Brain emits all tool calls for a step as single tool_calls event. TUI responds with single tool_results event. Exactly one JSONL round-trip per rpc_loop iteration. Serialized execution would be 3x slower. Brain controls round-trip count.

data.title: Typed ADT Boundary: ToolCallReq as ADT Not String Discriminant
data.rationale: ToolCallReq MUST be algebraic data type. parse_tool_calls is single translation point from stringly-typed JSON to typed ADT. After boundary, all dispatch uses ADT pattern matching. Eliminates string-dispatch problems.

data.title: Net Effect Removed from rpc_loop
data.rationale: Brain no longer makes HTTP calls. Effect signature becomes {AI, SharedMem, IO, Env, FS, Clock} — honest description of reasoning. env_client.ail deleted. AgentState loses env_url field.

data.title: model_change Commands Survive Blocked read_tool_results
data.rationale: read_tool_results loops on model_change while blocked waiting for tool_results. Applies SharedMem update and recurses. No silently dropped model_change commands during tool execution.

data.title: ParseToolCalls ADT: NoCalls | Calls | ParseError
data.rationale: Malformed attempted tool JSON not treated as completion. Empty [] = NoCalls. Unsupported tools preserved as warnings with corrective feedback. ParseError for malformed JSON.

data.title: Payload-Safe Think-Span Filtering (Not Global Stripping)
data.rationale: Compute <think>...</think> spans as index ranges. Build JSON candidates in source order. Drop candidates inside think spans. No byte mutation — preserves WriteFile.content with literal <think> text. First valid candidate accepted. Global stripping would corrupt tool payloads.

data.title: Quote-Aware Fence Scanner for Embedded Backticks
data.rationale: Replace naive split(rest, '```') with quote-aware closing-fence scan. Track in_string and escaped state. Treat ``` as closing fence only when in_string==false. Keeps embedded code fences inside JSON string literals intact.

data.title: Strict Single-Match Default with Explicit replace_all Opt-In
data.rationale: replace_all=false requires exactly one match. replace_all=true requires at least one match and replaces all. Fail on ambiguous match unless explicitly opted in. Prevents accidental multi-site edits.

data.title: Optimistic Hash Guard (expected_sha256) for Staleness Detection
data.rationale: Optional expected_sha256 field. If present and file hash differs, fail before any edit. Prevents edits against concurrently changed files.

data.title: Atomic Write: Temp File + fsync + Rename
data.rationale: Write full new content to temp file in same directory, fsync, rename over target. No partial writes if any edit in batch fails. Target file unchanged if final rename doesn't happen.

data.title: Ordered Edit Application Against Current Working Content
data.rationale: Edits applied in order against current file content. Read file once, validate preconditions, apply all edits, compute diff, write atomically unless dry_run. Read-before-edit policy enforced.

data.title: Three-Layer Test Strategy: Inline + Properties + Z3
data.rationale: Layer 1: inline tests with concrete LLM output patterns (parse.ail). Layer 2: property tests with 100 random inputs, auto-shrinking (prompts.ail, agents_md.ail). Layer 3: Z3 verification for simple invariants (is_root length bound, dirname shrinking). Complements not replaces.

data.title: Test Suite as Semantic Drift Signal for Self-Modification
data.rationale: Candidate brain must pass all inline+property+Z3 tests. Changed behavior requires test updates — intentional changes are visible in diff. Prevents silent behavioral drift during self-modification.

data.title: Dafny Tier 2: Verification-Aware Specs with Counterexample Feedback
data.rationale: Brain generates Dafny mirror functions with requires/ensures/decreases clauses. Dafny calls Z3 automatically; failures produce concrete counterexamples with specific input values. Qualitative difference from Lean 4 unsolved goals — brain can fix bugs from counterexamples.

data.title: Lean 4 Tier 3: Full Structural Induction (Research Bet)
data.rationale: Brain generates theorem statements + tactic blocks. Full induction over unbounded data. Equivalence proofs between old/new implementations. Advisory gate during research phase — budget exhaustion → warning not rejection. Removable via LEAN4_VERIFY env var.

data.title: Cost Escalation Ladder: Cheapest Tier First
data.rationale: Z3 (ms) → Dafny (1-10s) → Lean 4 (2-60s). Property verified at cheaper tier never reaches more expensive tier. Not parallel redundant checks — ordered fallback.

data.title: oh-my-pi File Tools via Delegated Channel (Path 3)
data.rationale: Replace native AILANG file tools with oh-my-pi's TypeScript+Rust implementations routed through delegated-tool channel. AILANG runtime keeps agent loop control. Only execution backend moves to TS. Hashline anchors, fuzzy edit pipeline, Rust N-API performance.

data.title: Phase 0: Migrate TypeScript Frontend from Node.js to Bun
data.rationale: oh-my-pi targets Bun throughout. Hashline uses Bun.hash.xxHash32 — no Node.js equivalent. Bun runs TypeScript directly, eliminating tsc compile step and Jest ESM workarounds. All Node built-ins and npm deps supported by Bun.

data.title: Per-Model Edit Mode Defaults: Hashline for Frontier, Replace for Gemma
data.rationale: Frontier models default hashline for staleness detection. Mid-tier hashline with auto-fallback. Gemma 4 defaults replace mode — fuzzy pipeline is the real win; avoid anchor confusion. Adaptive fallback tracks consecutive failures and downgrades.

data.title: TOML-based config system with layered precedence
data.rationale: Provide discoverability, grouping, and project-specific defaults for 49+ env vars. TOML supports comments and nested tables; JSON lacks comments.

data.name: TypeScript config loader
data.description: Loads .motoko/*.toml files and flattens to env vars

data.title: JSON profile config with CLI arg overrides for headless operation
data.rationale: Enable fully headless supervisor invocation without TypeScript TUI; config from JSON profiles + CLI args; only secrets via env vars.

data.name: AILANG config module
data.description: Parse CLI args, load JSON profiles, merge defaults

data.title: Named config profiles under .motoko/config/<profile>/ directory
data.rationale: Support A/B testing and reproducible benchmarks with named profiles (default, benchmark, debug); use MOTOKO_CONFIG env var for selection.

data.title: Move config ownership to AILANG core; JSON profile files; supervisor as entry point
data.rationale: Motoko core should own runtime config semantics instead of TypeScript; switch from TOML to JSON (AILANG has no TOML parser); supervisor.ail becomes primary entry point.

data.name: AILANG supervisor
data.description: Primary entry point for headless and TUI-driven execution

data.title: TUI-layer retry on 429 with exponential backoff
data.rationale: Capture runtime stderr, emit synthetic error event, retry on 429 with backoff; no runtime changes needed.

data.title: Add Result-returning AI ops to runtime; retry in AILANG with backoff
data.rationale: Process stays alive on AI errors; retry happens in-process with full history; policy in AILANG, not buried in Go or TUI.

data.name: AILANG AI effect operations
data.description: Add callResult, callJsonResult returning structured error records

data.title: Merged plan combining runtime Result ops with builtin registration and stdlib wrappers
data.rationale: Best version: prevents crashes, implements retry in AILANG, preserves conversation history, keeps existing APIs stable.

data.title: Add AIError type and Result-returning AI stdlib wrappers
data.rationale: Enable core/rpc.ail to branch on retryable vs non-retryable errors; bounded retry with backoff; preserve backward compat for existing call* APIs.

data.title: Guard prose-only responses that indicate future tool intent
data.rationale: Avoid premature 'done' when model says 'I will now use Search...'; detect continuation intent phrases and inject corrective feedback instead.

data.title: Sanitize thinking traces from conversation history; keep in TUI display and logs
data.rationale: Avoid replaying raw thinking in subsequent model calls; preserve thinking for display; reduce context bloat; prevent prompt leakage.

data.title: Real-time LLM output streaming from runtime to TUI for OpenAI models
data.rationale: Users see answer text incrementally; preserve existing final thinking/done flow; implement as typed streaming effect.

data.name: OpenAI provider streaming
data.description: Chat Completions and Responses API streaming implementation

data.name: TypeScript runtime process spawner
data.description: Spawn and manage AILANG subprocess

data.title: Add low-cost, reliable experiment tracking for GRPO/SFT training using MLflow OSS
data.description: Use file-backed MLflow tracking in Modal volume for structured run metadata, artifact storage, and queryable experiment history without SaaS lock-in.
data.rationale: Current tracking split across terminal logs, manual notes, markdown reports. MLflow gives structured params/metrics/tags, artifact storage, and queryable history.

data.title: Use MLflow as observability backbone for Motoko runtime sessions
data.description: Session/turn/tool-call telemetry via MLflow with correlation IDs, metrics, and redacted artifacts without runtime instability.
data.rationale: End-to-end session tracing with correlated events enables debugging and performance analysis

data.title: Implement Motoko MLflow Observability Phase 1
data.description: Telemetry schema, instrumentation points, MLflow writer, redaction, feature flags
data.rationale: Phase 1 execution target for end-to-end session tracing

data.title: Implement MLflow Tracking for GRPO/SFT Training
data.description: Add mlflow_tracker module, wire dependency, integrate train/eval logging, update docs
data.rationale: Phase 1 execution for experiment tracking instrumentation

data.title: Add RunPod as alternative execution backend while keeping Modal unchanged
data.description: New RunPod worker entrypoint and scripts, keep Modal path untouched, shared core provider-agnostic
data.rationale: Enable non-Modal hardware choices without replacing existing Modal workflow

data.title: Implement RunPod Additive Integration Phase 1
data.description: Worker script, serverless handler, launch scripts, env template, README updates
data.rationale: Phase 1 execution for alternative backend support

data.title: Prove GRPO improves Gemma 4 AILANG code generation using compiler as reward signal
data.description: Use ailang check/run/verify as reward functions, ~30 training prompts, 4 reward tiers
data.rationale: Baseline 62.7% compile, 58.7% pass rate with 90.4% compile errors. GRPO with compiler reward directly targets failure mode.

data.title: Adapt little-coder's benchmark system to evaluate Motoko
data.description: Python RPC client spawns Motoko as subprocess, reads JSONL events, runs language test suites
data.rationale: little-coder's harness is agent-independent. Only RPC layer and subprocess spawn need to change for Motoko.

data.title: Add benchmark-level control for Gemma 4 thinking mode via provider options
data.description: Thread --thinking on/off/auto through benchmark runner -> Motoko RPC -> AI request options -> OpenAI payload
data.rationale: Enable explicit benchmark control of thinking without changing prompt hacks

data.title: Empirically test if Gemma 4 writes F# materially better than AILANG
data.description: Run 42 problems in 3 conditions (AILANG baseline, F# unconstrained, F# constrained) to gate transpiler premise
data.rationale: Transpiler premise requires >70% F# pass rate vs baseline. Data-driven decision on F# transpiler viability.

data.title: Build CLI tool to convert markdown to audio using Qwen3-TTS
data.description: Local inference backend with chunked synthesis, deterministic concatenation, profile-driven defaults (realtime/longform)
data.rationale: Qwen3-TTS provides low-latency (12Hz) and long-form (25Hz) tracks. Local-first avoids API dependencies.

data.title: Enable AILANG Agent to use any model on OpenRouter via OpenAI-compatible API
data.description: Thin shim using existing OpenAI client with WithBaseURL. Model string format: openrouter/<model-id>
data.rationale: OpenRouter exposes OpenAI-compatible endpoint. No new HTTP client code needed.

data.title: Support local/LAN OpenAI-compatible endpoints with auth-none and discovery
data.description: Add OPENAI_BASE_URL env var for local/self-hosted endpoints, support auth-none, optional model discovery in TUI
data.rationale: Enable use of local endpoints (vLLM, Ollama, DGX) without authentication while preserving cloud OpenAI

data.title: Implement purely functional chess engine in AILANG with Z3 verification
data.description: Bitboards, recursive move generation, Alpha-Beta pruning, formal verification of rules
data.rationale: Demonstrate AILANG capability for complex symbolic computation with formal correctness guarantees

data.name: MLflow Tracker Module
data.description: Safe wrappers for MLflow logging with non-fatal error handling

data.name: RunPod Worker Entrypoint
data.description: Pure Python CLI/dispatcher for RunPod environment

data.name: Reward Functions
data.description: code_extractable, ailang_check, ailang_run, ailang_verify reward tiers

data.name: Training Prompts
data.description: ~30 prompts covering easy/medium/hard AILANG tasks with expected outputs

data.name: Motoko RPC Client
data.description: Python RPC client for spawning and communicating with Motoko subprocess

data.name: AI Handler
data.description: Provider-specific request handling with Options transport

data.name: F# Language Runner
data.description: FSharpRunner for dotnet fsi execution and error classification

data.name: md2audio CLI
data.description: Command-line interface with profile and voice options

data.name: Qwen Backend Adapter
data.description: Model loader and synthesis function for Qwen3-TTS

data.name: AI Config Provider Routing
data.description: Provider type detection and API key handling

data.name: Chess Type Definitions
data.description: Piece, Color, Board, Move types

data.name: Chess Rules Module
data.description: Move generation and legality verification with Z3 contracts

data.title: Rename swe namespace to core
data.rationale: Reposition project identity from SWE-agent to agent harness research platform; make architecture intent legible in code

data.title: Backward-compatible SharedMem key migration
data.rationale: Preserve existing cached trajectories/model settings during namespace rename; fallback from core:* to swe:* on reads

data.title: Rewrite SYSTEM.md and AGENTS.md for harness identity
data.rationale: Agent should understand its own architecture (3 processes, 2 protocols, brain modules, constraints) rather than generic SWE instructions

data.title: Pre-flight inventory before mechanical migration
data.rationale: Classify all swe references by runtime-criticality to establish migration boundary

data.title: Agent self-knowledge via SYSTEM.md and AGENTS.md rewrite
data.rationale: Reduce operator burden by encoding architecture in docs the agent reads at startup

data.title: Surface OpenRouter reasoning field in thinking traces
data.rationale: Reasoning content from Qwen3 was silently dropped; add Reasoning field to chatMessage struct and prepend to response

data.title: ESC key for soft task interruption
data.rationale: Allow user to interrupt running task without exiting; re-enable input for follow-up prompt

data.title: Preserve in-flight history on abort
data.rationale: Avoid erasing assistant content when abort occurs; model can reference attempted work in follow-up

data.title: Enable deletion via OmnigraphMutate with guardrails
data.rationale: Remove missing delete tool friction; add delete queries with branch-name safety rails to prevent main-branch data loss

data.title: Bulk delete queries with naming-based guardrail
data.rationale: Support cleanup of PoC data; enforce cleanup/* or wipe/* branch naming to prevent accidental main-branch wipes

data.title: AILANG as composition language for multi-step workflows
data.rationale: Replace sequential single-command execution with code-first composition; reduce LLM-step count and context window consumption

data.title: Effect-based capability control for AILANG snippets
data.rationale: Sandbox capabilities via effect annotations; declared effects must not exceed granted --caps

data.title: Filesystem sandbox via AILANG_FS_SANDBOX env var
data.rationale: Restrict all FS operations to workdir; prevent path traversal attacks in composition snippets

data.title: Type-check retry loop with targeted error feedback
data.rationale: Catch type errors before execution; allow LLM to self-correct from check errors without burning a step

data.title: Delegate AILANG composition to subagent
data.rationale: Isolate AILANG authoring/retry loops from main agent; reduce token cost and cognitive load; enable cheaper model tier for subagent

data.title: AILANG_COMPOSITION_MODE env var for inline vs subagent
data.rationale: Preserve existing inline path for rollback and A/B testing; new subagent path becomes default after validation

data.title: New Compose tool for high-level intent expression
data.rationale: Main agent emits structured tool call with intent, expected_output, hints instead of raw AILANG fence

data.title: Chunked JSONL event streaming from /compose endpoint
data.rationale: Separate main-agent context (clean history) from developer visibility (full subagent activity); stream events without entering main msgs

data.title: Deterministic semi-formal template routing
data.rationale: Route tasks to specialized semi-formal prompts (patch-eq, fault-loc, code-qa); gate on SEMI_FORMAL_ENABLED flag

data.title: Configurable step budgets for solver and verifier
data.rationale: Reserve verifier budget; control via AI_MAX_STEPS, AI_MAX_STEPS_SOLVER, AI_MAX_STEPS_VERIFIER env vars

data.title: Read-only tool policy for verifier pass
data.rationale: Prevent verifier from mutating state; limit to ReadFile, Search, and safe bash commands (cat, rg, find, sed -n)

data.title: Two-pass solver+verifier orchestration
data.rationale: Run solver pass with FullAccess; verifier pass with ReadOnly; inject findings if not equivalent; continue until completion or budget exhaustion

data.title: Verifier certificate JSON schema with semantic validation
data.rationale: Encode verifier output as structured JSON; validate premises, evidence, gaps, alternative-hypothesis coverage; downgrade to inconclusive if invalid

data.name: Type Definitions
data.description: Core types: AgentState, Msg, ToolCallReq, ToolResultItem, ToolPolicy

data.name: Tool Dispatch
data.description: Execute tool calls with policy enforcement; native tools and delegated tool dispatch

data.name: Omnigraph Mutations
data.description: GraphQL mutation queries for delete operations on decisions and components
